posts.title: The Regional Edit: Events Across the Triangle, Sandhills & Coast
posts.author: Core Collective Editorial
posts.excerpt: Weekly draft: 5–6 curated events across Central NC, military base communities, and coastal towns — plus an Eastern NC heritage spotlight.
posts.cover_alt: Community gathering along the North Carolina coast and piedmont
posts.body:
## Introduction

Your weekly **Regional Edit** for the week of **July 20, 2026** — curated happenings across the 130-mile Eastern NC arc from the Research Triangle through the Sandhills military communities to the Crystal Coast and Cape Fear.

## Featured Events

_Automated feeds returned limited listings this week. Review and enrich with local chamber, base MWR, and coastal tourism calendars before publishing._

**Suggested coverage zones:**
- **Central NC:** Raleigh-Durham metro festivals and markets
- **Military communities:** Fort Liberty (Fayetteville), Seymour Johnson AFB (Goldsboro), Camp Lejeune (Jacksonville)
- **Coastal towns:** Wilmington, New Bern, Morehead City, Emerald Isle

## 📜 Eastern NC Heritage & Hidden Gem

**Smithfield & Johnston County** — Smithfield flourished as a Johnston County rail and agricultural crossroads in the late 1800s, linking Raleigh markets to coastal supply chains along what is now US-70. The corridor's I-42 designation signals long-term commuter growth for buyers priced out of Wake County.

## Considering a Move?

From PCS relocations to coastal second homes, Core Collective advisors guide buyers across the Triangle, Sandhills, and coast. Connect for a personalized community tour.

posts.title: Eastern NC & Triangle Regional Market Briefing
posts.author: Core Collective Editorial
posts.excerpt: Regional market draft: luxury trends, PCS relocation flows, coastal investment demand, and I-40/I-95/US-70 corridor growth across Eastern NC.
posts.cover_alt: Eastern North Carolina regional skyline and growth corridors
posts.body:
## Market Overview

Eastern North Carolina regional briefing for the week of **July 20, 2026** — covering luxury housing dynamics, PCS relocation flows, coastal investment demand, and commute-corridor growth across the 130-mile Raleigh radius.

## Luxury Housing Trends

- **Raleigh-Durham Metro** — Luxury inventory above $750K remains tight in North Hills, Cary, and Apex; staged listings continue to see competitive offers within the first 10 days.
- **Sandhills & Military Markets** — Fayetteville, Goldsboro, and Jacksonville offer materially lower basis than Wake County with strong rental demand from PCS and contractor households.
- **Coastal Premium** — Wilmington, New Bern, and Carteret County barrier-island markets show sustained second-home and retiree demand with supply constraints on water-adjacent parcels.

## PCS Military Relocation Buyer Flows

Fort Liberty (Fayetteville), Seymour Johnson AFB (Goldsboro), and Camp Lejeune (Jacksonville) drive recurring turnover cycles that create predictable absorption in the $300K–$600K executive segment. Buyers extending tours or converting BAH to equity increasingly look 30–45 minutes off-base for newer construction and school access.

## Coastal Investment Demand

Emerald Isle, Morehead City, and Wilmington capture distinct investor profiles: vacation rental yield on Bogue Banks, maritime-industry rentals in Carteret County, and long-term appreciation along the Cape Fear corridor. Triangle equity migration via I-40 continues to support coastal basis growth.

## Commute Corridor Growth (I-40 · I-95 · US-70 / I-42)

Johnston County (Clayton, Smithfield), Wayne County (Goldsboro), and Wilson–Greenville connectors benefit from US-70 and I-42 narratives that shorten Raleigh access. Remote and hybrid RTP workers increasingly underwrite "regional lifestyle" searches across this arc rather than limiting to Wake County.

## 📍 Submarket History & Defense Corridor Spotlight

**I-40 / US-70 / I-95 Commute Corridors** — Johnston County (Smithfield, Clayton) and Wayne County (Goldsboro) benefit from I-42 and US-70 widening narratives that shorten effective Raleigh commutes. Defense-sector and RTP remote workers increasingly map "90-minute lifestyle" searches across this 130-mile arc rather than limiting to Wake County.

*Connect with Core Collective for a personalized regional market briefing spanning the Triangle, Sandhills, and coast.*

posts.title: Q1 2026 Triangle Market Analysis: Luxury Inventory Trends
posts.author: Marsha Watson
posts.excerpt: Median luxury home prices in Raleigh-Durham rose 6.3% year-over-year as inventory tightened across North Hills and Cary. Here's what buyers and sellers need to know this quarter.
posts.body:
## Market Overview

The Triangle luxury segment continues to outperform national averages, driven by sustained in-migration from coastal metros and Research Triangle Park expansion.

## Key Takeaways

- **North Hills & North Raleigh** — Median list prices up 8.1% YoY with 1.4 months of inventory.
- **Cary & Apex** — Strong demand for new construction; multiple-offer scenarios remain common above $750K.
- **Durham** — Investment activity steady; cap rates holding at 5.5–6.8% for Class B multi-family.

## Looking Ahead

Sellers with properly staged, accurately priced listings are still seeing competitive offers within the first week. Buyers should prioritize pre-approval and off-market access through a Core Collective advisor.

*Connect with our team for a personalized market briefing.*

posts.title: The Weekend Edit: Top Triangle Community Events This Month
posts.author: Sylvia T. Wheeler
posts.excerpt: From North Hills wine festivals to Chapel Hill gallery openings — your curated guide to the best local events for Triangle residents and newcomers.
posts.cover_alt: Crowd enjoying an outdoor community festival
posts.body:
## Introduction

Triangle living means more than exceptional homes — it's a vibrant community culture that makes relocation feel like coming home.

## Featured Events

- **North Hills Art & Wine Walk** — Midtown Raleigh's premier spring showcase of local galleries and vintners.
- **Downtown Raleigh Food Hall Series** — Monthly chef collaborations celebrating NC farm-to-table cuisine.
- **Chapel Hill Jazz Festival** — Three nights of world-class performances on Franklin Street.

## Considering a Move?

Experiencing these neighborhoods firsthand is the best way to find your perfect fit. A Core Collective advisor can arrange private tours and community introductions tailored to your lifestyle.
